title: Gone Abie Gone

jokes.text: Couch gag parody of Wacky Races with car names like 'The Piggly Wiggums', 'The Greed Hornet', 'Hick Dastardly'
jokes.setup: Classic Wacky Races opening setup with race cars
jokes.punchline: Character names punned into car names (Chief Wiggum becomes Piggly Wiggums, etc.)
jokes.explanation: Solid visual parody with clever wordplay combining character names with vehicle names. Economy is good - multiple puns delivered rapid-fire.

jokes.text: Beat ya! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Racing competition conclusion
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated, though predictable in context

jokes.text: Pushy sign! Don't tell me what to try!
jokes.setup: Homer encountering a promotional sign
jokes.punchline: Treating inanimate sign as personally offensive authority figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his relationship with authority extends to signage

jokes.text: I don't recall that.
jokes.setup: Marge reminds Homer that Dr. Hibbert told him to eat healthier
jokes.punchline: Homer's convenient selective memory
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer denial, well-executed but familiar territory

jokes.text: You got your brother following me? Chester needs a job. I paid him to build a shed! Where is my shed, Bernice?
jokes.setup: Bernice knows about Dr. Hibbert's restaurant visit
jokes.punchline: Rapid escalation from surveillance to employment to unfinished construction
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity revealing marital dynamics through mundane details

jokes.text: Can I borrow ten bucks from the cash register? I've gotta get drunk before a party.
jokes.setup: Employee asking to borrow money
jokes.punchline: Completely honest about using it for pre-party drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - brutally honest about his dysfunction with casual delivery

jokes.text: A homeless man is giving himself a sink bath!
jokes.setup: Customer reporting a hygiene emergency
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of deeply uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that establishes Moe's as a dive bar

jokes.text: This one's cold.
jokes.setup: Homer crying out about worst pain imaginable
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to mundane complaint about temperature
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of dramatic moment with perfect Homer priority shift

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, your injury was no accidénte, it was... negligénce.
jokes.setup: Legal professional explaining case
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic Spanish-accented pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Mild accent humor, competent but not particularly clever

jokes.text: They have healed much too quickly. Sit still while I give your head the 'third degree.'
jokes.setup: Lawyer concerned about quick healing
jokes.punchline: Burns reference combined with interrogation pun
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting literal burns with figurative investigation

jokes.text: Yes, these are special acid markers.
jokes.setup: Homer screaming in pain from markers
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that lawyer is using actual acid
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation revealing lawyer's complete lack of ethics

jokes.text: It wasn't even my place! Man, I got a bad lawyer.
jokes.setup: Krusty forced to pay settlement
jokes.punchline: Blames his lawyer despite winning the case
jokes.explanation: Good irony - winner complaining about successful outcome

jokes.text: Well, thought bubble Marge said we should put it in a college fund for Lisa.
jokes.setup: Bart asking what Homer will do with money
jokes.punchline: Homer references his own thought process as 'thought bubble Marge'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging cartoon visual conventions while revealing Homer's psychology

jokes.text: That place is great! On their wall, they had a photo with an old couple walking on the beach with their pants rolled up.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he likes the bank
jokes.punchline: Completely misses the point of financial advertising imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - attracted to banks for completely wrong reasons

jokes.text: I saw a Sesame Street about it! Kermit was wearing his trench coat and everything. Wait, the frog in the trench coat is Kermit, too? All the frogs on that show are Kermit. Keeps all the other frog actors out of work.
jokes.setup: Carl explaining banking through Sesame Street reference
jokes.punchline: Escalating realization about Kermit's monopoly on frog roles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from banking explanation to entertainment industry critique via muppet labor practices

jokes.text: A poker site is now safer than an American bank. Has our nation-- built on people suing because their onion rings were too hot-- come to this?
jokes.setup: Moe suggesting poker site for money storage
jokes.punchline: Ironic lament about national decline from a bar owner suggesting gambling
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary with good irony about source of patriotic concern

jokes.text: Has our nation-- built on people suing because their onion rings were too hot-- come to this?
jokes.setup: Moe reflecting on America's current state
jokes.punchline: Defining America as fundamentally based on frivolous lawsuits
jokes.explanation: Excellent satirical take on litigious American culture. The specific onion ring example makes it more vivid and funny.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey, hey! Don't you badmouth this country. Compared to the rest of the Third World, we're doin' great!
jokes.setup: Moe criticizing America
jokes.punchline: Barney's patriotic defense accidentally insults America worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic patriotism - defending country by comparing it to developing nations

jokes.text: I've had it for a long time. I was just about to throw it out, and then this happened. Mwah!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking where Homer got his lawsuit-themed shirt
jokes.punchline: Homer's obvious lie about pre-existing shirt followed by kiss
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer transparent lie, competently executed but predictable

jokes.text: That's one way to avoid drunk driving. Another way is don't drink. I'm not Superman.
jokes.setup: Homer considering walking to avoid drunk driving
jokes.punchline: Presents not drinking as impossibly heroic feat
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - equating basic self-control with superhuman ability

jokes.text: Ha! Of course I do! Happy anni... birth... entine... Shark week?
jokes.setup: Marge testing if Homer remembers what yesterday was
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly desperate guessing ending with TV programming
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Homer's panic and pop culture-addled brain

jokes.text: What the...?! Stupid calendar. First you put the Fourth of July on Sunday, and now this!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he forgot to visit Grampa
jokes.punchline: Blames calendar for poor scheduling of fixed holidays
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - blaming calendar for inconvenient placement of national holiday

jokes.text: Then when he wants us to take him out to lunch, point to the leftovers, tell him we already did, but he forgot.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining plan to apologize to Grampa
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals plan to gaslight elderly father
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elder abuse presented matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Your father's not going to be around forever. Marge, you take that back! Fine, he'll be around forever. You take that back!
jokes.setup: Marge making reasonable point about Homer's father's mortality
jokes.punchline: Homer's impossible demands about his father's lifespan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's inability to accept any uncomfortable truth

jokes.text: Missing?! What kind of a prison are you people running here? If it's any consolation, we'll probably find him when the river thaws in the spring. A lot of bobbers then.
jokes.setup: Homer outraged that nursing home lost his father
jokes.punchline: Nurse's casual discussion of finding bodies in spring
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation about nursing home body count, delivered with inappropriate cheer

jokes.text: This room is like a museum of meaningless old crap. By which I mean every museum.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing Grampa's belongings as meaningless
jokes.punchline: Extends critique to all museums
jokes.explanation: Sharp escalation from personal dismissal to cultural institution critique

jokes.text: Flimsy lead, take me away!
jokes.setup: Homer finding a potential clue about his father
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on following weak plot devices
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about plot conventions while staying in character. Very economical and self-aware.

jokes.text: I'm only down $62. Not counting my tips to the waitresses.
jokes.setup: Homer downplaying his gambling losses
jokes.punchline: Reveals additional losses to virtual waitresses
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing Homer's complete inability to understand online gambling

jokes.text: Four queens! Yes! Yes...! Gambling, eh? Satan's most potent recruiting tool!
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating a winning hand
jokes.punchline: Flanders' immediate moral judgment appearing at worst moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character integration - Flanders arriving at moment of temptation

jokes.text: Yeah! $50 up! Time to play with house money! Or time to walk away from the table. Shoo! How did he...?
jokes.setup: Homer torn between gambling and Flanders' advice
jokes.punchline: Confusion about Flanders' omnipresence
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up to previous Flanders appearance, playing with his role as conscience

jokes.text: Can't let Lisa lose her sense of wonder.
jokes.setup: Homer gambling with Lisa's college fund
jokes.punchline: Rationalizing it as protecting her innocence
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic rationalization. Homer protecting Lisa's 'wonder' while destroying her future is perfectly selfish.

jokes.text: We don't like questions, man. Yeah, but I'm just trying to get information, like a nosy reporter or an undercover cop.
jokes.setup: Tough guys suspicious of Homer's questions
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing himself to exactly what they're worried about
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - reinforcing their suspicions while trying to allay them

jokes.text: When people ate steak, drank champagne, and the music... oh, the music! I hated the music!
jokes.setup: Spiro nostalgically describing the good old days
jokes.punchline: Subverts expected nostalgia with honest complaint
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection subverting nostalgic restaurant owner trope

jokes.text: Simpson! I pay you to clean tables, not to write songs. And you, Marvin Hamlisch, I pay you to write songs, not to clean tables! What can I say? I like to pitch in.
jokes.setup: Boss berating employees for wrong tasks
jokes.punchline: Famous composer working as busboy purely out of helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist cameo with real composer doing menial labor by choice

jokes.text: My dad was an aspiring songwriter like Charles Manson?
jokes.setup: Learning about Grampa's musical aspirations
jokes.punchline: Compares songwriting to serial killer's failed career
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor connecting failed ambition to true crime

jokes.text: Oh, that's right, 'cause it's a train wreck.
jokes.setup: Spiro asking why he left Greece
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of entire country's economic situation
jokes.explanation: Topical economic humor delivered with appropriate immigrant perspective

jokes.text: Tell him sorry from Spiro Papadapaconstanti-kasgianopolop odopotopolis. / Got it. Papadapaconstanti-kasgianopolop odopotopolis.
jokes.setup: Spiro giving his extremely long Greek name
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow remembering and repeating the impossible name perfectly
jokes.explanation: Standard ethnic name joke with subversion of Homer actually remembering it. Somewhat lazy ethnic humor but has character surprise.

jokes.text: Papadapaconstanti-kasgianopolop odopotopolis. Got it. Papadapaconstanti-kasgianopolop odopotopolis.
jokes.setup: Spiro giving his long Greek surname
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident but impossible repetition
jokes.explanation: Standard ethnic name humor, competent but relies on familiar trope

jokes.text: There's exactly one Rita Lafleur living in Springfield! That's where we live!
jokes.setup: Lisa finding one person with that name in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization of obvious coincidence
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer slow-on-the-uptake moment, functional but predictable

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! I get two Christmases!
jokes.setup: Learning his father has been married to another woman
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate focus on material benefits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priority inversion - family scandal becomes holiday bonus

jokes.text: Time is money. Money is money. And money is college, which can lead to more money someday, but who knows anymore.
jokes.setup: Jennifer Tilly giving poker advice
jokes.punchline: Economic anxiety undermining motivational platitude
jokes.explanation: Good escalating pessimism about education's economic value

jokes.text: The man I looked up to my whole life. You never looked up to him.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming disappointment in his father
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate correction of obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Marge calling out Homer's revisionist history

jokes.text: You never looked up to him. / Well, it's a good thing I didn't because I'd be pretty devastated right now.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Homer's lack of respect for his father
jokes.punchline: Homer finding a silver lining in his own neglect
jokes.explanation: Good Homer rationalization of his own character flaws. Perfect selfish logic turning neglect into wisdom.

jokes.text: Beneath his mediocrity there was genius. Then a layer of anger. Then a beautiful soul. Then some more anger! A lot of layers.
jokes.setup: Rita explaining attraction to Grampa
jokes.punchline: Psychological onion metaphor revealing mostly anger
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating metaphor for complex but mostly negative personality

jokes.text: I love men on the rebound. They always think they've done something wrong and they usually come with furniture.
jokes.setup: Rita explaining attraction to recently separated men
jokes.punchline: Practical benefits of dating divorced men
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about dating psychology with practical twist

jokes.text: My Dad had furniture?! I don't know him at all!
jokes.setup: Rita mentioning divorced men come with furniture
jokes.punchline: Homer's shock at his father's material possessions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's surprise at his father's hidden depths

jokes.text: He can handle 110 volt, but 220 would kill him.
jokes.setup: Explaining why young Homer can't go to Europe
jokes.punchline: Treating electrical voltage like child safety issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant old man logic connecting European travel to electrical incompatibility

jokes.text: This is why 'life' comes before 'love' in the dictionary.
jokes.setup: Grampa choosing responsibility over romance
jokes.punchline: False wisdom based on alphabetical accident
jokes.explanation: Excellent fake wisdom connecting philosophical priority to dictionary order

jokes.text: Move over, Fonzie. I've got a new hero.
jokes.setup: Homer moved by his father's sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Replacing the Fonz as his role model
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's pop culture-filtered emotions. The Fonzie reference dates the joke but fits his personality.

jokes.text: What does that mean? I got super-addicted to heroin.
jokes.setup: Rita's cryptic musical metaphor about life
jokes.punchline: Blunt revelation about drug addiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from artsy metaphor to harsh reality

jokes.text: It's like Kenny Rogers' most famous line: 'This is a bad piece of chicken.'
jokes.setup: Homer quoting Kenny Rogers about knowing when to fold
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong song quote about food quality
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer misremembering of famous gambling song

jokes.text: No gambling story has a happy ending except Seabiscuit. But you never hear about the ruined lives of the people who bet against him.
jokes.setup: Marge comforting Lisa about gambling losses
jokes.punchline: Subverts feel-good horse racing story with losers' perspective
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of inspirational sports narrative from losers' viewpoint

jokes.text: I was Ivy! Strong Ivy!
jokes.setup: Lisa devastated by loss of college prospects
jokes.punchline: References her botanical self-identification
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific breakdown using plant metaphor

jokes.text: Because I actually like you and felt sorry for you. What?! You did? Forget I said anything!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why Bart helped her
jokes.punchline: Bart's embarrassment at admitting sibling affection
jokes.explanation: Sweet sibling moment with appropriate Bart defensiveness

jokes.text: Because I actually like you and felt sorry for you. / What?! You did? / Forget I said anything!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why Bart helped her
jokes.punchline: Bart's embarrassment at revealing genuine care
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing Bart's hidden affection. Predictable but well-executed character moment.

jokes.text: After people found out it's 98% squirrel sweat, they sorta stopped buying.
jokes.setup: Explaining why cologne is only sold at one store
jokes.punchline: Grotesque ingredient revelation
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor explaining product's limited availability

jokes.text: Man, you're making me hot for my Dad.
jokes.setup: Rita describing young Grampa's appeal
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate attraction to father's past
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but character-appropriate response to romantic nostalgia

jokes.text: Oh, I know him. He's a man who has no son!
jokes.setup: Store clerk asking Abe if he knows the man in the photo
jokes.punchline: Abe dramatically disowning Homer while Homer stands right there
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony with Abe's theatrical disowning. Perfect old-man dramatic flair while setting up the family reunion.

jokes.text: I didn't wander off, ya big fat salami! I ran away!
jokes.setup: Family thinking Abe got lost
jokes.punchline: Abe's indignant correction with food-based insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abe character moment with food-based insult and dramatic indignation. 'Big fat salami' is memorably specific.

jokes.text: It's just that in today's multichannel environment, you have too many great choices. Look at Sunday night. There's, like, eight amazing shows. None of them on Fox.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he doesn't visit Grampa more
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating jab at own network's programming
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor with show mocking its own network's quality

jokes.text: That's how wolves die in the wild.
jokes.setup: Watching elderly couple's romantic duet
jokes.punchline: Comparing romance to animal death
jokes.explanation: Dark but poetic observation about elderly romance and mortality

tmdb_overview: Homer and Marge try to track down Grampa by following clues that lead to secrets from his past.
